books.title: The AI Agent Revolution: How Autonomous Systems Are Reshaping Enterprise
books.author: Rakesh Gohel
books.why: Directly addresses the agentic AI transformation David has been tracking all week — from Salesforce Agentforce's $800M ARR to Visa's 20-bank agentic payments programme. Gohel combines the theoretical framework with enterprise case studies across banking and insurance, industries David's digest heavily covers.

books.title: Money Machine: How AI Payments Are Reshaping Global Finance
books.author: Melanie Swan
books.why: Directly relevant to today's Visa Agentic Ready story — 20+ European banks testing AI agent-initiated payments — this book examines how foundation models trained on transaction data unlock new financial services and reshape payment infrastructure at the institutional level.

stories.headline: OpenAI acquires Astral: uv, ruff, and ty Python tools join Codex workflow with 2M weekly users
stories.summary: OpenAI acquired Astral — the company behind uv, ruff, and ty, the most widely used open-source Python developer tools — to integrate them directly into Codex, which now has over 2 million weekly active users and 3x user growth since January. Astral's tools will remain open-source while being woven into the full agentic Python development workflow.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to guild system journeyman tools
stories.lateral_explanation: Medieval guilds granted journeymen access to shared tools that became part of the craft itself — the tools were not separate from the work, they were the standard of the work. When OpenAI acquires the tools Python developers use every day, it is inserting itself into the craft standard that defines

stories.headline: OpenAI announces desktop superapp: ChatGPT, Codex, and Atlas browser merge into one unified agent hub
stories.summary: OpenAI Chief of Applications Fidji Simo revealed that the company will consolidate ChatGPT, its Codex multi-agent coding app, and the Atlas AI-native browser into a single desktop application for macOS, ending the fragmented three-product approach that was stretching engineering resources. The superapp lets a user browse documentation, summarize it with ChatGPT, and have Codex propose code — all without switching apps.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Swiss Army knife design failure
stories.lateral_explanation: The Swiss Army knife is iconic but nobody uses one for serious work — specialists outperform it in every individual task. OpenAI's superapp is a bet that integration value beats specialist depth, which is only true when the tasks are co-dependent and the switching cost between tools is the real bott

stories.headline: ChatGPT for Excel enters beta: natural language spreadsheet creation, editing, and analysis directly in Excel
stories.summary: OpenAI launched a ChatGPT for Excel beta in the U.S., Canada, and Australia, enabling users to build, modify, and analyze spreadsheets through natural language directly inside Microsoft Excel. The integration extends GPT-5.4's document intelligence into the most widely used business spreadsheet tool, allowing non-technical users to create pivot tables and write formulas without leaving Excel.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to stenography as professional power
stories.lateral_explanation: In the early 20th century, stenography — the ability to translate spoken language into written records in real time — was a professional superpower that determined who could participate in business. ChatGPT for Excel is the inverse: translating intention into structured data without needing the tech

stories.headline: Google Personal Intelligence expands free to all U.S. Gemini users: Gmail, Photos, Chrome connected
stories.summary: Google made its Personal Intelligence feature available to all free U.S. Gemini users, letting Gemini draw on a user's Gmail, Google Photos, YouTube history, and Search activity to deliver personalized responses. The feature is opt-in, less than two months after its paid-only launch, and available across AI Mode in Search, the Gemini app, and Gemini in Chrome.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to commonplace book tradition
stories.lateral_explanation: The commonplace book — a Renaissance-era practice of copying passages, observations, and ideas into a single personal notebook — was the original personal intelligence layer: a mind's accumulated knowledge made searchable by the owner alone. Google Personal Intelligence is the algorithmic version: a

stories.headline: Alibaba launches Wukong: enterprise AI agent platform for document editing, approvals, and meetings across Slack, Teams, WeChat
stories.summary: Alibaba released Wukong on March 17 — an enterprise AI agent platform built on Qwen, developed by the DingTalk team — managing multiple agents through a single interface for document editing, multi-step approval workflows, meeting transcription, and research tasks. Currently in invitation-only testing, it plans to integrate Taobao and Alipay into the agent ecosystem.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to monkey king narrative trickster
stories.lateral_explanation: Wukong — the Monkey King from Journey to the West — is a trickster deity capable of transforming into 72 different forms to complete any task given by a superior. Naming an enterprise AI agent platform after him is not accidental: the product is designed to shapeshift across different business workf

stories.headline: Google Gemini powers Ask Maps and Immersive Navigation in Google Maps global rollout
stories.summary: Google rolled out Gemini-powered Ask Maps — natural language querying within Google Maps for recommendations and itineraries — alongside Immersive Navigation, which overlays AI-generated route guidance onto a photorealistic 3D view of the environment. The integration puts Gemini's conversational intelligence directly into the world's most-used mapping service at scale.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to portolan chart annotation practice
stories.lateral_explanation: Medieval portolan charts were navigational tools covered in handwritten annotations by sailors — marginal notes about currents, ports, and hazards that layered human knowledge onto geographic space. Ask Maps is the dynamic version: a living annotation layer where conversational AI overlays contextua

stories.headline: MIT AI reconstructs 3D objects from reflected Wi-Fi signals alone — new sensing modality for robots
stories.summary: MIT researchers published a system that uses reflected Wi-Fi signals from standard routers to reconstruct full 3D shapes of objects in a room without cameras or LiDAR, by training a generative AI on signal-shape correlation pairs. The system gives robots a sensing modality that works through walls, in the dark, and in conditions where cameras fail.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to bat echolocation navigation
stories.lateral_explanation: Bats do not see their environment — they build a 3D model of it from reflected sound waves at frequencies humans cannot hear, resolving detail fine enough to catch a moth in total darkness. MIT's Wi-Fi AI does structurally the same thing: reconstructing shape from reflected signal propagation throug

stories.headline: Obra/superpowers reaches 92,100 GitHub stars as #1 trending agentic skills framework — Shell-based, zero Python boilerplate
stories.summary: obra/superpowers — a Shell-based agentic skills framework — surged to the top of GitHub Trending with 92,100 stars and 7,300 forks in the week of March 18, outranking Python-heavy alternatives. The framework lets developers compose and chain AI agent behavior using shell scripts, making it unusually accessible without heavy infrastructure overhead.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to UNIX philosophy composability
stories.lateral_explanation: UNIX's founding design principle — small programs that do one thing well and can be piped together — was a rejection of monolithic complexity in favor of composable primitives. obra/superpowers reaching 92K stars by applying that same philosophy to AI agents suggests the developer community is tired

stories.headline: Memories.ai ships visual memory layer for wearables and robotics with NVIDIA: persistent spatial context across sessions
stories.summary: Memories.ai launched a visual memory technology with NVIDIA enabling wearables and physical robots to retain and recall visual context across sessions, addressing the core limitation of embodied AI — the inability to remember what was seen before. The system targets industrial robotics and AR wearable applications where physical continuity of memory is a hard requirement.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to method of loci memory palace
stories.lateral_explanation: The method of loci — the mnemonic technique used by ancient Greek orators — works by anchoring information to specific locations in an imagined spatial journey, exploiting the brain's native capacity for spatial memory. Memories.ai is encoding this principle into hardware: giving robots a spatial me

stories.headline: Red Hat launches BYOA: enterprise governance layer wraps any AI agent with security, observability, and lifecycle management
stories.summary: Red Hat announced its Bring Your Own Agent strategy with NVIDIA, enabling enterprises to take agents built on any framework — LangGraph, AutoGen, CrewAI, Salesforce Agentforce — and wrap them in Red Hat's production infrastructure enforcing identity management, sandboxed execution, tool-call governance, and observability. It positions Red Hat as the enterprise operations layer for the multi-framework agent world.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to franchise infrastructure model
stories.lateral_explanation: A McDonald's franchise does not dictate what a franchisee's staff enjoys eating — it dictates the kitchen layout, the safety protocols, the supply chain, and the quality checkpoints. Red Hat's BYOA is exactly this model applied to AI agents: you can bring whatever agent you built, but it will run in

stories.headline: Visa Agentic Ready: 20+ European banks — Barclays, HSBC, Revolut, Santander — test AI agent-initiated payments
stories.summary: Visa launched its Agentic Ready programme in Europe on March 17, enrolling 20+ issuing banks including Barclays, HSBC UK, Revolut, Banco Santander, Commerzbank, and Raiffeisen Bank International to test AI agent-initiated transactions in controlled production environments. The programme uses tokenization and biometric authentication to tie each agent-initiated payment to a real human authorization.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to power of attorney legal doctrine
stories.lateral_explanation: Power of attorney — the legal instrument by which one person authorizes another to act on their behalf in financial and legal matters — has existed in some form since Roman law. Visa's Agentic Ready is constructing the technical equivalent: a formal authorization framework that lets an AI agent act

stories.headline: Salesforce Agentforce hits $800M annual revenue run rate from 29,000 enterprise deals in its first 15 months
stories.summary: Salesforce closed fiscal year 2026 with Agentforce generating approximately $800M in annual revenue run rate and appearing in roughly 29,000 customer deals since its October 2024 launch, making it the fastest-scaling enterprise AI agent product by named customer count. CEO Marc Benioff reported AI agents now handle approximately 50% of customer service interactions at enterprise clients.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Venetian merchant republic franchise model
stories.lateral_explanation: The Venetian merchant republic did not grow by conquering territory — it grew by creating the commercial infrastructure that everyone else needed to trade. Salesforce's 29,000 deals in 15 months follow the same logic: it is not selling AI, it is becoming the standard operating infrastructure layer t

stories.headline: OpenAI refocuses 100% on enterprise coding and business customers, drops consumer-facing side projects
stories.summary: OpenAI Chief of Applications Fidji Simo stated internally that the company was spreading engineering resources across too many products and is now redirecting all focus toward Codex, the enterprise customer platform, and the desktop superapp strategy. The decision follows Anthropic winning 70% of first-time enterprise AI purchases in March, signaling OpenAI views enterprise coding as its primary competitive moat.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Steve Jobs 1997 product matrix simplification
stories.lateral_explanation: When Steve Jobs returned to Apple in 1997, the company was selling 40+ products across overlapping lines. He canceled all but four — and the resulting focus restored profitability within a year. OpenAI's announcement mirrors this exactly: too many apps, too many stacks, competing for engineering att

stories.headline: Meta considering 20% workforce reduction — approximately 15,000 roles — to offset $115–135B AI infrastructure spend
stories.summary: Meta is reportedly evaluating laying off 20% of its 79,000-person workforce to offset capital expenditure of $115–135 billion in 2026 driven by its Meta Superintelligence Labs and core infrastructure build-out. If enacted, this would be Meta's largest headcount reduction since 2022, and follows a pattern where AI infrastructure investment is funded directly by workforce contraction.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to railway age labor displacement patterns
stories.lateral_explanation: When railways replaced canal networks in 19th-century Britain, canal boat operators lost livelihoods — but the capital freed from labor costs funded the infrastructure that employed three times as many in rail construction and maintenance. Meta's calculus is the same: the workforce being reduced is

stories.headline: Agnes AI surpasses $20M ARR, targets Singapore Exchange listing — AI-native enterprise platform without human operators
stories.summary: Agnes AI, a Singapore-based enterprise AI platform that replaces human operators in workflows across finance, HR, and operations, announced it has surpassed tens of millions in ARR and is targeting a Singapore Exchange public listing by end of 2026. The company's growth tracks Singapore's national AI push and is one of the first AI-native platforms in Southeast Asia to approach public market scale.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to East India Company trading post expansion
stories.lateral_explanation: The East India Company's commercial expansion across Asia followed a pattern of replacing local trading intermediaries with a centralized, standardized commercial infrastructure — the Company became the operating system of trade. Agnes AI is doing this for knowledge work in Southeast Asia: replacing

stories.headline: World Tools for Humanity releases AgentKit: blockchain human verification for AI agent commerce
stories.summary: Tools for Humanity released AgentKit on March 20, a toolkit enabling merchants and platforms to verify that a real human authorized an AI agent purchase before completing the transaction. The system integrates blockchain-based World ID identity proofs into the agentic payment flow, allowing AI agents to shop autonomously while maintaining human accountability at the point of authorization.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to wax seal authentication in medieval contracts
stories.lateral_explanation: Medieval commercial contracts required the personal wax seal of the principal as proof that a specific human had authorized the transaction. AgentKit is the cryptographic version: before an AI agent completes a purchase, it presents a World ID proof — the equivalent of the wax seal — that traces bac

stories.headline: Encyclopedia Britannica and Merriam-Webster sue OpenAI for reproducing 100,000+ copyrighted articles via RAG
stories.summary: Encyclopedia Britannica and Merriam-Webster filed a joint copyright infringement lawsuit against OpenAI, alleging the company scraped nearly 100,000 copyrighted articles and that OpenAI's RAG workflow reproduces substantial portions of their content in generated responses. The suit directly targets RAG as a production deployment pattern rather than model training, with broad implications for enterprise AI systems.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Gutenberg press and book guild conflict
stories.lateral_explanation: When Gutenberg's press began reproducing manuscripts at scale in the 1450s, the hand-copying guilds of monastic scriptoria faced an existential threat with no legal framework to protect them. The Britannica and Merriam-Webster lawsuit is structurally identical: encyclopedic knowledge aggregators who

stories.headline: Unitree G1 humanoid robot returns tennis shots with 96% forehand accuracy using LATENT on just 5 hours of training data
stories.summary: Researchers published results from the LATENT system on a Unitree G1 humanoid robot achieving 96% forehand shot success in rally tests after training on just five hours of motion capture data using simulation-to-real transfer. The result marks a new threshold in how quickly physical robots can reach reliable sport-level precision with minimal data.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to proprioceptive learning in child development
stories.lateral_explanation: A child learning to return a tennis shot goes through thousands of failed attempts over months, building proprioceptive feedback loops between eye, arm, and racket that no instruction manual can shortcut. LATENT compresses this into five hours of motion data by doing what simulation-to-real transfer

stories.headline: Nexthop AI raises $500M Series B for AI-optimized data center networking — Lightspeed and a16z lead
stories.summary: Nexthop AI closed a $500M Series B led by Lightspeed Venture Partners with Andreessen Horowitz joining, to build AI-optimized networking infrastructure for data centers running enterprise AI agent workloads. As agent workloads grow in volume and real-time latency requirements, network architecture becomes a constraint alongside GPU compute, which Nexthop directly targets.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to road infrastructure and industrial revolution
stories.lateral_explanation: The steam engine could generate power before Britain had roads capable of moving goods at industrial scale — the bottleneck was not energy production but distribution infrastructure. Nexthop AI's thesis is that GPU compute is now ahead of network infrastructure: agents are generating more inter-node

stories.headline: Synopsys builds multi-agent framework for semiconductor design on NVIDIA Nemotron — first chip-design agentic stack
stories.summary: Synopsys — the dominant EDA software company for chip design — announced a multi-agent framework built on NVIDIA Nemotron and the NeMo Agent Toolkit, enabling AI agents to assist semiconductor engineers across verification, synthesis, and simulation tasks. This is the first production multi-agent deployment in the EDA space, applying agentic AI to the design of the hardware that will run future AI.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to self-referential Escher drawing
stories.lateral_explanation: M.C. Escher's Drawing Hands depicts two hands drawing each other into existence — a perfect loop of self-reference with no clear starting point. Synopsys deploying AI agents to design semiconductor chips is the same loop made real: AI is now designing the silicon that will run AI, creating a recursi

stories.headline: FanziMad 2026: 153 artistas y 4.000 visitantes este fin de semana en la Biblioteca Iván de Vargas
stories.summary: La cuarta edición de FanziMad — el festival de fanzines, autoedición y cómic de Madrid — ocupa este fin de semana (20-22 de marzo) la Biblioteca Iván de Vargas con 153 artistas, talleres de linograbado, mesas redondas y el regreso del concurso FanziMad con premios en metálico. Con entrada libre y 4.000 asistentes esperados, consolida Madrid como capital española de la autoedición independiente.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to samizdat underground publishing
stories.lateral_explanation: Samizdat — the Soviet-era practice of self-publishing and hand-distributing censored texts outside state control — was a cultural resistance built on the physical act of reproduction: typewriters, carbon paper, mimeograph machines. FanziMad inherits this ethos: fanzines are the direct descendant of

stories.headline: Carmen Linares 'Antología, 30 Aniversario' esta noche en Circo Price: tres décadas de cante condensadas en un programa
stories.summary: Esta noche Carmen Linares presenta en el Teatro Circo Price su Antología 30 Aniversario, reuniendo las formas de cante — soleá, seguiriya, bulería, fandango, taranta — que han definido sus tres décadas de carrera con nuevos arreglos y músicos de referencia. La actuación coincide con el equinoccio de primavera, abriendo la temporada musical.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to anthology as literary form
stories.lateral_explanation: The anthology — from the Greek anthología, a gathering of flowers — was originally a poetic form in which a single volume collected the best poems of many authors across many eras, arguing through selection that certain voices were worth preserving together. Carmen Linares's Antología inverts this:

stories.headline: 'Allá, en el mundo de la verdad': ocho funciones gratuitas sobre Valle-Inclán en la Comunidad de Madrid
stories.summary: La Comunidad de Madrid programa ocho representaciones gratuitas de Allá, en el mundo de la verdad, un espectáculo teatral que recrea una entrevista con Valle-Inclán en su último período madrileño, permitiendo al público escuchar al escritor argumentar su estética del esperpento y su visión de España. La pieza complementa la exposición de la Biblioteca Regional inaugurada el 20 de marzo.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Boswell's Life of Johnson interview method
stories.lateral_explanation: James Boswell's biography of Samuel Johnson — published in 1791 — was revolutionary because it was built not from written records but from thousands of hours of recorded conversation: Boswell was essentially running a live interview documentary two centuries before the format existed. Ana Contreras

stories.headline: Teatralia 2026, tercera semana: circo contemporáneo internacional en espacios de la Comunidad de Madrid
stories.summary: El festival Teatralia llega a su tercera semana con compañías de circo contemporáneo internacional que trabajan en el cruce entre acrobacia, narrativa física, danza aérea y teatro de objetos, en espacios de la Comunidad de Madrid con entrada libre. La edición de 2026 explora cómo el circo del siglo XXI recupera la tradición del espectáculo popular y la transforma en un lenguaje escénico de autor.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to commedia dell'arte improvisation tradition
stories.lateral_explanation: Commedia dell'arte — the Italian street theater tradition that gave Europe its first professional actors in the 16th century — worked by combining fixed character types with completely improvised physical action, creating a theater that was simultaneously structured and unpredictable. Contemporary c

stories.headline: La Orquesta Mondragón celebra 50 años en Madrid: rock de cabaré y las crónicas musicales de la Movida
stories.summary: La Orquesta Mondragón — fundada por Javier Gurruchaga, que llevó el rock de cabaré al Madrid de la Movida — celebró su 50 aniversario el 20 de marzo con un programa que recorrió sus álbumes más emblemáticos y los textos que convirtieron sus canciones en crónicas culturales del período democrático. Es uno de los pocos grupos de la Movida activos con su formación original.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Brecht cabaret as political form
stories.lateral_explanation: Bertolt Brecht developed his Lehrstücke — learning plays — for the Weimar-era cabaret precisely because the form mixed entertainment with pointed social commentary in a setting where the audience was drinking and off-guard. Javier Gurruchaga's use of cabaret in post-Franco Madrid follows the same po
